text: Apple announced a new MacBook Pro with M4 chip at their annual developer conference.
The Lakers defeated the Celtics in overtime with a buzzer-beater three-pointer.
The Senate voted to approve the new infrastructure bill with bipartisan support.
The new Marvel movie broke box office records in its opening weekend globally.
Tesla's autonomous driving system passed regulatory approval in three new countries.
Serena Williams announced her comeback to professional tennis after a two-year break.
The president signed an executive order on climate policy reform and emissions targets.
Taylor Swift's world tour sold out within minutes in every city on the schedule.
Google released a major update to Android with improved privacy features and AI tools.
The World Cup final drew a record television audience of over 2 billion viewers.
Congress is debating a new cybersecurity bill that would require mandatory reporting.
The Grammy Awards ceremony featured surprise performances and unexpected winners.
Microsoft acquired a leading AI startup for over $10 billion in its largest deal.
Olympic swimmers set three new world records during the qualifying heats today.
The prime minister announced snap elections following the collapse of the coalition.
A new streaming series became the most-watched show in Netflix history this week.
Researchers developed a quantum computer that solved a problem in minutes not years.
The marathon world record was broken by over thirty seconds in perfect conditions.
Trade negotiations between the two countries reached a critical impasse this week.
The Oscars honored diverse storytelling with wins for international film categories.
A new smartphone app uses AI to translate conversations in real-time between languages.
The baseball season opens with several teams favored for the championship this year.
Voter turnout reached a historic high in the local municipal elections yesterday.
The new video game launch attracted millions of players within its first 24 hours.
SpaceX successfully launched and landed its largest rocket on the first attempt.
The football transfer window saw record spending across European leagues this season.
The supreme court ruling on privacy rights has wide implications for digital data.
A legendary rock band announced their farewell world tour starting next summer.
New battery technology promises to double electric vehicle range within two years.
The championship boxing match ended in a controversial split decision late Saturday.
The UN General Assembly voted on a resolution addressing global food insecurity.
Broadway shows saw record ticket sales during the holiday weekend performance run.
Intel unveiled its next-generation processor architecture at the technology summit.
The tennis grand slam tournament delivered upsets as top seeds were eliminated early.
A political scandal emerged involving leaked documents and classified information.
The music festival announced its lineup featuring over 200 artists across six stages.
A startup raised $500 million to develop brain-computer interface technology.
The rugby world cup draw placed defending champions in a challenging group.
New legislation aims to regulate social media platforms and protect minor users.
An acclaimed director's new film received a ten-minute standing ovation at Cannes.
